paper.title: 591 high velocity stars in the Galactic halo selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: Extraction covers only the 20 fastest HiVelSCs (Hivel1–Hivel20) for which individual unbound probabilities and full data are provided in the printed tables (Tables 1, 2, 6, 7, 8). The remaining 571 HiVelSCs from the full 591-object catalog are documented in candidate_groups_considered because the external CSV catalog is not among the provided source files and individual unbound status cannot be extracted from the paper alone.

method_chain.summary: Overview of the two main input data sources used in this work: LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectroscopic survey (10,608,416 spectra) and Gaia DR2 astrometry, photometry, and covariance matrices.

source_refs.context: Section 2.1 description of LAMOST and Gaia

method_chain.summary: Cross-match LAMOST DR7 with Gaia DR2 using a 5 arcsec radius via TOPCAT, producing the LAMOST-Gaia catalog of over 10 million entries.

Select HiVelSC sample: require positive parallax, fractional parallax error f_varpi <= 0.2; for multi-epoch spectra keep highest S/N; visually inspect RV consistency. Resulting in 591 candidates. Subsequently apply zero-point corrected LASP RV (step-04) and recalculate V_GC.

source_refs.context: Sample selection and RV checks in Section 2.3

method_chain.summary: Adopt LASP RV for all HiVelSCs because LASP errors include observation/processing uncertainties. Apply zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s determined from cross-match with Huang+2018 RV standard stars.
Adopted solar position and motion: distance to GC R0 = 8.2 kpc, Sun offset above disk z0 = 25 pc, circular velocity of LSR 238 km/s, solar peculiar velocity [U, V, W] = [14.0, 12.24, 7.25] km/s.

source_refs.context: Assumptions for distance and velocity determination

method_chain.summary: Compute heliocentric distance by inverting Gaia parallax (d = 1/varpi). Then compute Galactocentric Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z) and distance r_GC using the solar position from step-05. Monte Carlo 1000 realizations with the full Gaia covariance matrix and Gaussian RV uncertainty; adopt median and 16th/84th percentiles.
inputs: Gaia DR2 astrometry (parallax, positions, proper motions, covariance)

method_chain.summary: Calculate Galactocentric total velocity V_GC and Cartesian velocity components (Vx, Vy, Vz) from proper motions, corrected RV, and solar motion using the Monte Carlo approach. The Galactic Cartesian coordinate system: X-axis from Sun to GC, Y in direction of Galactic rotation, Z towards North Galactic Pole.

Adopt seven Galactic potential models: Watkins+2019, Gnedin+2005, Kenyon+2014, Koposov+2010, Paczynski+1990, MWPotential2014 (Bovy 2015), Xue+2008. These are used to compute escape velocities at each star's position.

Compute escape velocity V_esc at each star's Galactocentric position for each of the seven potential models, using the potential functions and r_GC from step-06. This step only produces the escape velocity values, without computing unbound probabilities (see step-17).

Perform numerical orbit integration backwards in time using the Galpy package with the MWPotential2014 model. For each star, 1000 MC realizations, trace back 10 Gyr with 10 Myr time step. Compute orbital parameters: eccentricity e, maximum height Zmax, minimum disk crossing radius r_min, and energy difference E-Phi(infinity).

Classify HiVelSCs into four origin types from the computed probabilities: P_gc and P_MW derived from r_min (step-10) and P_ub from step-17 (the classification is described after step-17, but this step captures the classification criteria). Apply thresholds from Table 4: HVS (P_gc > 0.16), HRS (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5, P_ub > 0.5), RS (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW > 0.5, P_ub < 0.5), OUT (P_gc < 0.16, P_MW < 0.5).

Determine atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) and alpha-element abundance [alpha/Fe] using two independent pipelines: LASP (LAMOST Stellar Parameter Pipeline) and DD-Payne (data-driven Payne). Values for the 20 fastest are listed in Table A3.

Quality flag 'astrometric_flag' defined to mark stars with conservative high-quality Gaia astrometry, using criteria from Lindegren+2018 (Appendix C) and Marchetti+2019 (Section 4). 476 HiVelSCs satisfy this flag.

Gaia DR2 astrometry and photometry: positions, proper motions, parallaxes, G/BP/RP magnitudes, and covariance matrix. Provides the fundamental astrometric and photometric measurements used throughout the work.
LAMOST DR7 low-resolution spectroscopy: radial velocities (LASP) and stellar atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]), plus spectral classification and S/N_r.

Derive origin probabilities from the orbital integration of step-10: P_gc is the fraction of MC orbits with r_min < 1 kpc, P_MW the fraction with r_min < 25 kpc. These probabilities are used in candidate classification.
Compute unbound probability P_ub as the fraction of 1000 MC realizations where V_GC > V_esc for each potential model, using the V_GC distributions from step-07 and escape velocities from step-09.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel1 is a hyper-runaway star candidate (HRS) with unbound probability 0.996 under the Watkins+2019 potential. It is among the 20 fastest HiVelSCs in the sample.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: All values taken directly from printed tables; unbound probability >0.5 and origin classification is listed in Table A2.

inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Direct values from printed tables; unbound probability >0.5 in all models.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Hivel9 is one of the 20 fastest HiVelSCs. It has an unbound probability >50% (Pub(W)=0.804) under the Watkins+2019 potential model, and is classified as HRS (hyper-runaway star). Included as a high-velocity star candidate possibly unbound from the Galaxy.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Data taken directly from the paper's tables for the 20 fastest HiVelSCs; unbound probabilities clearly stated.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The full sample of 591 HiVelSCs selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2, of which only the 20 fastest are printed in the paper's tables. The remaining 571 are documented only in an external CSV catalog (http://paperdata.china-vo.org/LYB/lamostdr7_gaiadr2_hvs_591.csv) not among the provided source files. According to the paper, 43–304 of these may be unbound from the Galaxy depending on the adopted potential model.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Individual data and unbound probabilities for 571 candidates are not accessible because the full catalog is an external CSV file not provided among the source files; only the 20 fastest HiVelSCs (Hivel1–Hivel20) printed in the paper are included as individual candidates.

source_refs.context: Description of the 591 HiVelSCs and the external catalog URL
